page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is a compute-optimized instance powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-based execution.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% via stress-ng testing. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with the Passmark memory score reaching the top 10% tier. Cryptographic capabilities are also robust, supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instructions, which yield strong results in CPU encryption tests. Multi-core compute, memory bandwidth, and web serving throughput consistently perform within the average range.

The server exhibits strong capabilities in image processing and small-to-medium scale LLM text generation, where it achieves top-tier and strong performance respectively. However, processing larger models, such as a 70B parameter LLM, reveals performance tradeoffs, resulting in weak prompt processing speeds. Given its high-density memory performance and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-bound workloads that do not require local SSDs or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, image processing, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 16 physical cores. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached resources. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier memory performance and strong single-core CPU execution, alongside strong cryptographic and image processing capabilities. Multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and web serving throughput remain in the average tier. While highly capable for small-to-medium language model text generation, it shows performance tradeoffs with larger 70B models. This configuration represents a balanced, cost-efficient option for dedicated compute tasks, making it suitable for software compilation, image processing, web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering top-tier memory performance and strong single-core execution.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-32 features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, offering strong single-core and top-tier memory performance.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance delivering top-tier memory performance and strong single-core execution for balanced, compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with dedicated core allocation.
Equipped with 128 GB of RAM, achieving top-tier memory performance benchmarks.
Strong single-core CPU execution and hardware-accelerated cryptography.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and medium-scale LLM inference.
